items.title: Pipeline preprocessing inside CV
items.avoid: Scaling or imputing the full matrix X before calling cross_val_score.
items.prefer: sklearn Pipeline chaining preprocessors with the estimator; CV refits the whole chain per fold.
items.why: Prevents validation statistics from leaking into training transforms.

items.title: Report mean and spread
items.avoid: Publishing only the mean CV score without fold-level detail.
items.prefer: Log per-fold metrics and std (or confidence interval from repeated CV) alongside the mean.
items.why: High spread flags unstable models and supports honest comparison when means are close.
items.example: Model A: mean AUC 0.842, std 0.011; Model B: mean 0.845, std 0.048 — prefer A for deployment stability.

items.title: Match CV strategy to data structure
items.avoid: Default shuffled k-fold for grouped users, time series, or spatial blocks.
items.prefer: GroupKFold, TimeSeriesSplit, or other splitters that mirror how unseen production data arrives.
items.why: Exchangeable k-fold assumes i.i.d. rows; violating that yields optimistic CV scores.
items.example: Same patient in train and val folds → use GroupKFold with group=patient_id.

items.title: Separate development CV from final test
items.avoid: Retuning on the test set because CV and test scores disagree slightly.
items.prefer: Use CV (or nested CV) for all development decisions; touch the test set once for sign-off.
items.why: Repeated test peeking converts the test set into a validation set and biases the final estimate.
items.example: Pick ridge α by CV on train+val; refit on train+val; report RMSE once on locked test.
